annotations.label: Stochastic (property)

anchor.snippet: is the property of being well-described by a random probability distribution

annotations.note: The informal property of being random/probabilistic is not a single Mathlib declaration — Mathlib formalizes specific probabilistic structures (measures, kernels, processes) rather than a blanket 'stochastic' predicate.

annotations.label: Stochastic process (Khinchin)

anchor.snippet: the first mathematical definition of a stochastic process as a family of random variables indexed by the real line

annotations.note: Mathlib treats stochastic processes informally as functions `T → Ω → E` and has predicates like `IsKolmogorovProcess` and `MeasureTheory.Filtration` over them, but no standalone `StochasticProcess` definition matching Khinchin's general formulation.

annotations.label: Stochastic matrix / Markov process

anchor.snippet: a stochastic matrix , which describes a stochastic process known as a Markov process

annotations.note: Stochastic matrices are formalized as `Matrix.rowStochastic` (and `doublyStochastic`), and Markov kernels exist via `ProbabilityTheory.IsMarkovKernel`, but the link to a Markov process built from a stochastic matrix is not packaged as one declaration.

annotations.label: Brownian motion as continuous-time stochastic process

anchor.snippet: One of the simplest continuous-time stochastic processes is Brownian motion

annotations.note: Mathlib has the finite-dimensional Gaussian projective family of real Brownian motion in `Mathlib.Probability.BrownianMotion`, but the continuous-time Brownian motion process itself is not yet constructed as a single decl.

annotations.label: Monte Carlo method

anchor.snippet: The Monte Carlo method is a stochastic method

annotations.note: No Mathlib declaration contains 'MonteCarlo' or formalizes the Monte Carlo numerical method.
